title: crocus × asturicus
intro: Crocus × asturicus, also known as the Asturian crocus, is a hybrid of the crocus flower and is often considered a subspecies of Crocus tommasinianus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Crocus × asturicus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and provide relief from symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a powder or made into a tea, and it can also be used as a natural dye due to its vibrant purple color.
